#!/usr/bin/env bash
# pi-failsafe — run pi with ONLY the ollama-cloud extension loaded.
#
# Why: any loaded extension can read ctx.modelRegistry (API keys) and
# intercept provider requests/responses. To keep the Ollama Cloud API
# key private, the only safe "failsafe" is to make sure NOTHING else
# can load. This launcher guarantees that:
#
#   1. ~/.pi/agent/extensions/*   -> quarantined (no global auto-discovery)
#   2. .pi/extensions/* (project) -> ignored via --no-approve (no trust)
#   3. settings.json `packages`   -> filtered to ollama-cloud only
#   4. settings.json `extensions` -> dropped (no explicit paths)
#   5. CLI -e/--extension         -> NOT passed (don't add one yourself)
#
# The ONLY thing that loads is the ollama-cloud package extension, which
# is what registers/uses the key anyway. Everything is restored on exit.
# If a previous run crashed and left failsafe state behind, the next
# invocation detects it, restores your normal config, and exits so you
# can re-run intentionally.
#
# Usage:  pi-failsafe [any pi args...]
#         pi-failsafe --restore    # force-restore normal config, don't launch
#         pi-failsafe --status      # show failsafe state, don't launch

set -uo pipefail

PI_AGENT_DIR="${PI_AGENT_DIR:-$HOME/.pi/agent}"
EXTDIR="$PI_AGENT_DIR/extensions"
QUARANTINE="$PI_AGENT_DIR/.extensions.failsafe-disabled"
SETTINGS="$PI_AGENT_DIR/settings.json"
SETTINGS_BAK="$PI_AGENT_DIR/.settings.failsafe.bak"
SETTINGS_TMP="$PI_AGENT_DIR/.settings.failsafe-tmp"
LOCKFILE="$PI_AGENT_DIR/.failsafe.lock"

log()  { printf '\033[1;33m[pi-failsafe]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;31m[pi-failsafe]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

# ---------- helpers ------------------------------------------------------
# Move every entry (files, dirs, dotfiles) from $1 into $2.
move_all_into() {
  local src="$1" dst="$2"
  mkdir -p "$dst"
  find "$src" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -print0 2>/dev/null |
    while IFS= read -r -d '' item; do
      local name; name="$(basename "$item")"
      if [ -e "$dst/$name" ]; then
        mv "$item" "$dst/${name}.failsafe-conflict" 2>/dev/null || true
      else
        mv "$item" "$dst/$name" 2>/dev/null || true
      fi
    done
}

restore_state() {
  # Returns 0 if there was leftover state to restore, 1 otherwise.
  local found=0
  if [ -d "$QUARANTINE" ]; then
    found=1
    log "Restoring quarantined extensions -> $EXTDIR"
    move_all_into "$QUARANTINE" "$EXTDIR"
    rmdir "$QUARANTINE" 2>/dev/null || warn "Could not remove $QUARANTINE (check it)"
  fi
  if [ -f "$SETTINGS_BAK" ]; then
    found=1
    log "Restoring settings.json from backup"
    mv -f "$SETTINGS_BAK" "$SETTINGS"
  fi
  rm -f "$SETTINGS_TMP" 2>/dev/null || true
  return $found
}

# ---------- self-heal: recover from a previous crashed run --------------
if [ "${1:-}" = "--restore" ]; then
  restore_state && log "Restored." || log "Nothing to restore."
  exit 0
fi

if [ "${1:-}" = "--status" ]; then
  if [ -d "$QUARANTINE" ] || [ -f "$SETTINGS_BAK" ]; then
    log "FAILSAFE STATE IS ACTIVE (a run is in progress or crashed):"
    [ -d "$QUARANTINE" ] && log "  quarantined extensions: $(find "$QUARANTINE" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -printf '%f\n' 2>/dev/null | tr '\n' ' ')"
    [ -f "$SETTINGS_BAK" ] && log "  settings.json backup present"
    log "Run 'pi-failsafe --restore' to force-restore normal config."
  else
    log "No failsafe state. Normal config is active."
  fi
  exit 0
fi

if [ -d "$QUARANTINE" ] || [ -f "$SETTINGS_BAK" ]; then
  warn "Leftover failsafe state from a previous (crashed?) run detected."
  restore_state || true
  warn "Your normal configuration has been restored."
  warn "Re-run 'pi-failsafe' to start a new failsafe session. Aborting."
  exit 0
fi

# ---------- lock against concurrent failsafe runs -----------------------
exec 9>"$LOCKFILE"
if command -v flock >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if ! flock -n 9; then
    warn "Another pi-failsafe run is already active (locked: $LOCKFILE)."
    exit 1
  fi
fi

# ---------- cleanup bookkeeping -----------------------------------------
SWAPPED_SETTINGS=0
MOVED_EXTENSIONS=0
CLEANED=0

cleanup() {
  [ "$CLEANED" = "1" ] && return
  CLEANED=1
  if [ "$MOVED_EXTENSIONS" = "1" ] && [ -d "$QUARANTINE" ]; then
    log "Restoring extensions -> $EXTDIR"
    move_all_into "$QUARANTINE" "$EXTDIR"
    rmdir "$QUARANTINE" 2>/dev/null || warn "Could not remove $QUARANTINE"
  else
    rm -rf "$QUARANTINE" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
  if [ "$SWAPPED_SETTINGS" = "1" ] && [ -f "$SETTINGS_BAK" ]; then
    log "Restoring settings.json"
    mv -f "$SETTINGS_BAK" "$SETTINGS"
  fi
  rm -f "$SETTINGS_TMP" 2>/dev/null || true
  if [ "$MOVED_EXTENSIONS" = "1" ]; then
    if [ -d "$EXTDIR/baz" ]; then
      log "OK: extensions restored (baz/ is back)."
    else
      warn "extensions restored, but baz/ missing — verify $EXTDIR"
    fi
  fi
}

# Ignore SIGINT/QUIT in the wrapper: pi handles Ctrl+C (cancel op) and
# Ctrl+\ itself; we don't want the wrapper dying mid-run and orphaning pi.
trap '' INT QUIT
trap cleanup EXIT
trap 'cleanup' TERM HUP

# ---------- preflight: ollama-cloud must be configured ------------------
[ -f "$SETTINGS" ] || { warn "No $SETTINGS found. Aborting."; exit 1; }
grep -qi 'ollama' "$SETTINGS" || {
  warn "ollama-cloud not found in $SETTINGS."
  warn "Install it first:  pi install git:github.com/fgrehm/pi-ollama-cloud"
  exit 1
}
AUTH="$PI_AGENT_DIR/auth.json"
if [ -f "$AUTH" ] && ! grep -qi 'ollama' "$AUTH"; then
  warn "No ollama-cloud key found in $AUTH — ollama-cloud may not authenticate."
  printf 'Continue anyway? (y/N) ' >&2
  read -r ans || ans=""
  case "$ans" in y|Y) ;; *) warn "Aborting."; exit 1;; esac
fi

# ---------- sanitize settings.json (keep only ollama packages) ----------
decision="$(python3 - "$SETTINGS" "$SETTINGS_TMP" <<'PY'
import json, sys
path, tmp = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2]
try:
    with open(path) as f:
        data = json.load(f)
except Exception as e:
    print("ERR:%s" % e); sys.exit(0)
pkgs = data.get("packages") or []
kept = [p for p in pkgs if isinstance(p, str) and "ollama" in p.lower()]
needs_swap = ("extensions" in data) or (len(kept) != len(pkgs))
if not needs_swap:
    print("CLEAN"); sys.exit(0)
sanitized = dict(data)
sanitized["packages"] = kept
sanitized.pop("extensions", None)
try:
    with open(tmp, "w") as f:
        json.dump(sanitized, f, indent=2); f.write("\n")
    print("SWAP")
except Exception as e:
    print("ERR:%s" % e); sys.exit(0)
PY
)"

case "$decision" in
  CLEAN)
    rm -f "$SETTINGS_TMP" 2>/dev/null || true
    log "settings.json already ollama-only (no swap needed)."
    ;;
  SWAP)
    cp -a "$SETTINGS" "$SETTINGS_BAK"
    mv -f "$SETTINGS_TMP" "$SETTINGS"
    SWAPPED_SETTINGS=1
    log "settings.json sanitized: only ollama-cloud package kept (backup at $SETTINGS_BAK)."
    ;;
  *)
    warn "Failed to sanitize settings.json ($decision). Aborting before any changes."
    rm -f "$SETTINGS_TMP" 2>/dev/null || true
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# ---------- quarantine global auto-discovered extensions -----------------
had_entries=0
if [ -d "$EXTDIR" ]; then
  # Is there at least one entry?
  if [ -n "$(find "$EXTDIR" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -print -quit 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
    had_entries=1
  fi
fi

if [ "$had_entries" = "1" ]; then
  entries="$(find "$EXTDIR" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -printf '%f ' 2>/dev/null)"
  move_all_into "$EXTDIR" "$QUARANTINE"
  MOVED_EXTENSIONS=1
  log "Quarantined: $entries"
else
  log "No global extensions to quarantine."
fi

# ---------- verify failsafe state, then launch ---------------------------
log "Verifying failsafe state before launch:"
if [ -d "$EXTDIR" ] && [ -n "$(find "$EXTDIR" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -print -quit 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
  warn "  $EXTDIR is NOT empty — failsafe compromised. Aborting (cleanup will restore)."
  exit 1
fi
log "  $EXTDIR is empty  -> no global extensions will load"
log "  --no-approve      -> no project-local extensions/packages/settings will load"
log "  settings packages -> $(python3 -c 'import json;d=json.load(open("'"$SETTINGS"'"));print(d.get("packages") or [])' 2>/dev/null || echo '?')"
log "Only the ollama-cloud extension will have access to the Ollama API key."
log "Starting pi (args: $*)."

set +e
pi --no-approve "$@" 9>&-
PI_RC=$?
set -e

log "pi exited (code $PI_RC). Restoring normal configuration."
exit $PI_RC

    rm -f "$SETTINGS_TMP" 2>/dev/null || true
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# ---------- quarantine global auto-discovered extensions -----------------
had_entries=0
if [ -d "$EXTDIR" ]; then
  # Is there at least one entry?
  if [ -n "$(find "$EXTDIR" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -print -quit 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
    had_entries=1
  fi
fi

if [ "$had_entries" = "1" ]; then
  entries="$(find "$EXTDIR" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -printf '%f ' 2>/dev/null)"
  move_all_into "$EXTDIR" "$QUARANTINE"
  MOVED_EXTENSIONS=1
  log "Quarantined: $entries"
else
  log "No global extensions to quarantine."
fi

# ---------- verify failsafe state, then launch ---------------------------
log "Verifying failsafe state before launch:"
if [ -d "$EXTDIR" ] && [ -n "$(find "$EXTDIR" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -print -quit 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
  warn "  $EXTDIR is NOT empty — failsafe compromised. Aborting (cleanup will restore)."
  exit 1
fi
log "  $EXTDIR is empty  -> no global extensions will load"
log "  --no-approve      -> no project-local extensions/packages/settings will load"
log "  settings packages -> $(python3 -c 'import json;d=json.load(open("'"$SETTINGS"'"));print(d.get("packages") or [])' 2>/dev/null || echo '?')"
log "Only the ollama-cloud extension will have access to the Ollama API key."
log "Starting pi (args: $*)."

set +e
pi --no-approve "$@" 9>&-
PI_RC=$?
set -e

log "pi exited (code $PI_RC). Restoring normal configuration."
exit $PI_RC
